turns.content: What brings you in today?
Hi, I came in because I have this rash on both my arms. It showed up yesterday, and it's really itchy. It's so bad that I can't sleep at night, and I feel tired all day from it.
When did this start?
It started yesterday morning. I woke up and noticed these red bumps on both my arms. Before that, my skin was totally normal, so it came out of nowhere.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The rash is mostly on my forearms, from my wrists up to a little below my elbows. It's not really painful, just very itchy—like a constant tickle that makes me want to scratch all the time. It feels worse when I'm trying to sleep or when I'm just sitting still.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills. Just the rash and the itching. But I do feel really tired, and my stomach has been a little upset, like I don't feel like eating much.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not taking any medications right now, not even for allergies or anything like that. I used a little bit of anti-itch cream I bought from the pharmacy last night, but it didn't help much.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic conditions like asthma or diabetes. I'm generally pretty healthy. I did have some stomach problems about a month ago, but that went away on its own. Nothing else.
Are you allergic to any medications?

I'm not sure about medicines, actually. I've never had a bad reaction to anything I've taken before, but I don't remember being tested for allergies. I think I'm fine, but I don't want to take any risks if it's not necessary.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I tried that anti-itch cream I bought without a prescription, but it didn't really stop the itching. I also put a cold towel on my arms to calm it down, and that helped for maybe 10 minutes, but then it came back just as bad.
Does anything make it better or worse?

The cold towel helps a little, but only for a short time. When I'm warm, like in bed or after a shower, the itching gets much worse. Scratching makes it redder and bumpier, so I try not to, but it's really hard.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been awful, honestly. I can't concentrate on my studies because the itching is so distracting, and I'm exhausted from not sleeping well. I'm also embarrassed to wear short sleeves in class because I don't want people to see the rash. I just want it to go away.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I don't smoke or drink alcohol. I've never really been into that stuff. I focus on my schoolwork and try to stay healthy, so I don't think that's related to this rash at all.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, not at all. My parents and my brother don't have any skin problems like this. My mom sometimes gets dry skin in the winter, but nothing like this red, itchy rash. So I'm not sure where this is coming from.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, I've never had a rash like this before. I've had mosquito bites and sometimes heat rashes in the summer, but those were much smaller and went away in a day or two. This one is different—it's on both arms and it's not going away.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, I think so. You explained about the rash and possible causes, but some words were a bit hard for me. Could you maybe go over the main points again, like what you think it might be and what I should do? I just want to be sure I understand everything.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's about a 7 or 8—the itching really bothers me a lot and I can't stop thinking about it. Emotionally, it's probably a 7 too, because I'm worried it might get worse or spread, and it's making me feel anxious and frustrated.
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, I have a couple of questions. First, what do you think caused this rash? And second, is there something I can do to stop the itching so I can sleep better? Also, if it doesn't go away, should I come back?
What do you think might be going on?

I'm not sure, honestly. I thought maybe it could be an allergic reaction, because I tried a new laundry detergent a few days ago. But I also ate some strawberries I'm not used to. What do you think it could be?
Is there anything else you think I should know?
I think that's all I can think of right now. I just really want this to go away soon because it's making me miserable. Thank you for listening and for answering my questions.
